entity.description:
Operational digital-twin node for a physical device, logical device, device group, subsystem, machine module, production cell, line, or plant node.
Device is the common runtime/control identity used by the model. It may represent a physical/logical device that has a corresponding PhysicalDevice row, or a group/system node that has a corresponding DeviceGroup row. The common Device layer owns status, active rule set, active rule, control method, operating mode, hierarchy, parameters, events and logs.
Hardware identity attributes such as serial number, UUID, hash, vendor, model and firmware belong to PhysicalDevice, not to Device, because a Device may also represent a group, subsystem, production cell, line or plant node.

field.name: parentDeviceId
field.description:
Optional parent in the Device hierarchy.
This parent-child hierarchy is used for system engineering structure: a plant may contain production cells, cells may contain subsystems, subsystems may contain groups, and groups may contain physical/logical devices. DeviceGroup/DeviceGroupMember can additionally describe explicit membership and role relationships for cooperating devices.

field.name: deviceTypeEnumId
field.description:
Device type of this runtime node.
For a physical/logical device this identifies the concrete or parent device class represented by this Device. For a DeviceGroup/system node it identifies the system/group type represented by the root node. Runtime services may compare this value with DeviceConfig.deviceTypeEnumId to verify whether a rule can apply a configuration to this target.

field.name: purposeEnumId
field.description: The device purpose.

field.name: controlMethodEnumId
field.description: The current control method used to control this device.

field.name: operatingModeEnumId
field.description: The device may be operating in initialization or homing mode or operational.

field.name: statusTypeId
field.description: The device can only runs status flows of this type.

field.name: statusFlowId
field.description: The current status flow used to control this device.

field.name: statusId
field.description: The current status item.

field.name: deviceRuleSetId
field.description:
Device rule set currently active for this device or system node.
For a physical/logical device this identifies the production cycle, batch procedure, diagnostic sequence or validation plan currently selected for that device. For a group/system node it identifies the active root plan for the represented subsystem, cell, line or plant area.

field.name: deviceRuleId
field.description:
Device rule currently active or last applied for this device.
It identifies the current phase/step within the active DeviceRuleSet. The corresponding rule determines which DeviceConfig is applied, checked, asserted, suggested or validated for the rule target.

field.name: coordinateSystemId
field.description: The coordinate system or reference frame used by this device. This overwrites the parent device coordinate system, if it exists. It may be overwritten by the coordinate system specified for each device or for trajectories.

field.name: transformationId
field.description: Process of transforming to the origin position of the coordinate system from a parent coordinate system using translation and rotation. At a minimum, a translation or a rotation must be defined for a transformation.

field.name: approximatedFunctionId
field.description: The trajectory currently running.

entity.description: A configured device event. It could generate a notification message in Moqui with topic, sub topic and message usually defined in CommonL10nData.xml.

field.name: eventPayload
field.description: Additional data or instructions related to the event.

field.name: isTriggerable
field.description: Can this event be triggered manually?

field.name: handlerServiceName
field.description: Service implementing the event handler.

entity.description: Technical information about a device describing its physical layout, functional characteristics, and relationships, trajectories, motion laws, etc. with other entities.

field.name: coordinateSystemId
field.description: The coordinate system or reference frame that this content assume as a reference.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: STEP ISO 10303 STEP AP203 or AP242 File
GDML Geometry Description Markup Language File
IGES Initial Graphics Exchange Specification File
X_T Parasolid XT Siemens Data Interchange File

entity.description: Aggregate lifetime statistics for a device.

field.name: meanTimeBetweenFailure
field.description: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) [hours]. MTBF is the average time the system operates without failure.

field.name: meanTimeToRepair
field.description: Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) [hours]. MTTR is the average time it takes to repair the device after a failure.

field.name: availability
field.description: Availability [ratio 0..1]. Availability measures the percentage of time the system is operational. Availability = MTBF/(MTBF + MTTR).

field.name: reliability
field.description: Reliability [ratio 0..1]. The reliability of the device over a period t can be modeled using the exponential reliability function: R(t) = exp(−1/MTBF). This gives the probability that the system will operate without failure for time t.

field.name: failureRate
field.description: Failure Rate (λ) [failures/hour]. The failure rate is the reciprocal of MTBF: λ = 1/MTBF.

field.name: meanTimeToAcknowledge
field.description: Mean Time To Acknowledge (MTTA) [hours]. Time taken to acknowledge a fault after detection.

field.name: overallEquipmentEffectiveness
field.description: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) ratio or percentage.

field.name: energyConsumption
field.description: Total lifetime energy consumption.

field.name: statusChangeCount
field.description: Total number of status changes of the device.

field.name: lastStatusChangeDate
field.description: Timestamp of the last status change.

field.name: totalOperatingTime
field.description: Total accumulated operating time of the device.

field.name: minTime
field.description: Minimum observed cycle or transition duration.

field.name: maxTime
field.description: Maximum observed cycle or transition duration.

field.name: avgTime
field.description: Average cycle or transition duration.

field.name: sumSquaredDuration
field.description: Sum of squared durations for variance calculation.

field.name: successfulOps
field.description: Count of successful operations.

field.name: failedTransitions
field.description: Count of failed state transitions.

entity.description:
Physical hardware identity associated with a Device.
PhysicalDevice contains attributes that identify the real machine, controller, drive, instrument, computer, gateway or other hardware asset: nameplate data, vendor/model/version data, serial number, UUID, hardware hash, location, cycle time and runtime platform details. These fields intentionally live here rather than on Device, because Device may also represent logical/group/system nodes that do not have one physical serial number.

field.name: deviceName
field.description: Name of a smart component or a piece of equipment.

field.name: uuid
field.description: Represents an immutable universally unique identifier (UUID) for the device.

field.name: hash
field.description: Condensed message digest from a secure one-way hash function.

field.name: cycleTime
field.description: The device cycle time in milliseconds. The cycle time is the time it takes to run the cyclic code start to finish once with interrupts or the interval between the completion of the reading of the data associated with that device until the beginning of the next sampling of that data. This information may be used by client software applications to understand how often information from the device is expected to be refreshed. The refresh rate for data from all child device entities will be the same as for the parent, unless specifically overridden.

field.name: isMulticore
field.description: The device has multicore support for symmetric multiprocessing CPUs.

entity.description:
DeviceGroup specialization for a Device that represents a group or system-engineering node.
A DeviceGroup may represent a group of cooperating physical/logical devices, a subsystem, a machine module, a production cell, a line or a plant area. The group itself is modeled as a Device so it can have status, dashboards, events, parameters and an active DeviceRuleSet, while DeviceGroupMember records describe the explicit members and their roles inside the group.
Group membership is part of the technical plant structure and should normally be authored by trusted seed data, engineering tools, administrators or AI-assisted technical generation rather than edited casually by a normal operator UI.

Membership row that places a device or nested device group inside a DeviceGroup.
The memberDeviceId identifies the participating Device. It may point to a physical/logical device or to another DeviceGroup when the plant structure uses nested groups. purposeEnumId and sequenceNum describe the member role and deterministic order within the parent group. Group membership is part of the system-engineering model and should normally be authored by seed data, an engineer, an administrator or an AI-assisted technical process, not edited casually by operators.

field.name: purposeEnumId
field.description: Device purpose in this group.

field.name: payload
field.description: Contains the aggregated JSON of the device's telemetry and data: { "temperature": 20, "rpm": 1000 ... }

entity.description: Represents a connection to a physical device.

field.name: userId
field.description: The user account used to access the device if it has authentication mechanisms.

relationship.description: No FK in order to allow externally authenticated users.

entity.description: Represents a request to a physical device.

field.name: deviceId
field.description: The physical device to which the request should be made.

field.name: requestGroup
field.description: A logical group containing a set of requests that are sent to the device, sequentially or concurrently, based on the priority. All requests within the same group could be executed in a single transition.

field.name: priority
field.description: Request execution priority, lower numbers run first among tasks that need to be run.

field.name: runServiceName
field.description: Service implementing the org.moqui.impl.BasicServices.run#DeviceRequestInternal interface, to execute this device request.

field.name: onlyChangedParameters
field.description: Only the parameters modified by the lastUpdatedStamp date will be sent or received. The EntityAuditLog is used to evaluate the changed parameters.

field.name: connectionName
field.description: The device connection used to run this request. For direct routing only.

field.name: brokerUri
field.description: If enabled, use this broker (MQTT/AMQP...). If null, use default/fallback from Moqui conf.

field.name: timeout
field.description: The maximum time in milliseconds to wait before timeout.

field.name: pollingInterval
field.description: Cyclical polling interval for direct subscription request.

field.name: qos
field.description: Request or message Quality of Service QoS. For broker-managed requests.

field.name: retained
field.description: Instructs the broker or Moqui to keep a copy of that request/message after sending it to current subscribers. For broker-managed requests.

field.name: query
field.description: The topic or contiguous address space to query physical items on the device. This field can be overridden by individual item query. The query should indicate the data type.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: Content Transfer Request (SFTP/file transfer of DeviceContent)

field.name: requestItemName
parameterId
field.description: The device parameter or register to request.

field.name: sequenceNum
field.description: Sequence number with which this item will be processed.

field.name: query
field.description: The topic or register address of a physical item. Generally it is the address of the register containing the physical item value on the device. This field overrides that of the DeviceRequest. The query should indicate the data type.

field.name: itemTypeEnumId
field.description: Item type (byte, word, integer, etc.). Protocol related physical value type.

field.name: minItemValue
field.description: Min item value, protocol related, to check for any disturbances, e.g. -10V, 0V, 0mA, 4mA, etc. For numeric types only.

field.name: maxItemValue
field.description: Max item value, protocol related, to check for any disturbances, e.g. 10V, 20mA, etc. For numeric types only.

field.name: defaultItemValue
field.description: The default item value. It is a protocol related physical value. Substitute value configured for output: after a bus failure is detected, failsafe will be activated and the output will be according to the configured substitute value.

field.name: itemValue
field.description: It can be used to store the current physical value if needed.

field.name: itemUomId
field.description: Item value unit of measure. Protocol related unit of measure, e.g. V, mA, etc.

field.name: allowDuplicate
field.description: Indicates whether duplicate values should be collected or avoided. For numeric parameter the corresponding value will be updated only when the change exceeds the tolerance configured. Example: If the temperature value fluctuates by 0.1°C each scan, the current snapshot will always be saved only when the change is greater than 0.5°C.

field.name: scalingFactor
field.description: Used to convert the reported value into the logical value.

field.name: significantDigits
field.description: Number of significant digits in the reported value.

field.name: reverseLogic
field.description: Reverse logic. Only applicable for text indicator parameter type.

field.name: activationDelay
field.description: The duration for which the physical signal must remain active to be considered stable. Useful for filtering or debouncing the signal. Only applicable for input text indicator or boolean parameter type. Difficult to apply with non real-time network communication protocols.

field.name: deactivationDelay
field.description: The duration for which the physical signal must remain inactive to be considered stable. Useful for filtering or debouncing the signal. Only applicable for input text indicator or boolean parameter type. Difficult to apply with non real-time network communication protocols.

field.name: format
field.description: Used to parse and format the output. Usually a bitmask.

field.name: mask
field.description: A bitmask for guiding and filtering input.

entity.description:
Reusable atomic device recipe/configuration.
A DeviceConfig is the declared source configuration for one compatible class of physical/logical devices: for example a PLC recipe, a drive macro, a tuning profile, a safety baseline, an emergency profile or an expected-state pattern used for validation. It stores declared Parameter values through Parameter.deviceConfigId.
DeviceConfig is not the live state of a machine. Live/materialized values are Parameter rows bound to Parameter.deviceId. A load/apply operation copies, maps or writes values from DeviceConfig scope into Device scope, after which the live values may diverge because of manual tuning, HMI changes, maintenance, automatic adaptation or emergency intervention.
A DeviceConfig may also bind a control method and/or trajectory, so it is more general than a scalar recipe: it can describe parameter values, the control method/profile and the motion/process trajectory to use. Composition across multiple devices is modeled by DeviceRuleSet and DeviceRule priority.

field.name: parentConfigId
field.description:
Optional parent DeviceConfig used for inheritance or versioned specialization.
A child configuration may represent a small override/diff from a base recipe, such as a customer variant, a tuning profile, a fault/emergency profile or a phase-specific profile. Runtime application must resolve the effective configuration according to the chosen inheritance strategy before materializing parameters.

field.name: configTypeEnumId
field.description: The type of device configuration.

field.name: purposeEnumId
field.description: Configuration purpose.

field.name: deviceTypeEnumId
field.description: Compatible DeviceType for this configuration. The value should identify a physical/logical device type, or a parent type whose nested children are also compatible. Runtime services may use the DeviceType hierarchy to verify that the target DeviceRule.deviceId can receive this DeviceConfig.

field.name: controlMethodEnumId
field.description: Control method used when this configuration is enabled.

field.name: approximatedFunctionId
field.description: Reference to a trajectory.

extend-entity.description:
Device-side Parameter scope extension. A Parameter entity bound to deviceConfigId is a declared recipe/configuration value. It belongs to the reference layer and is normally authored by seed data, an engineer, an administrator or an AI-assisted technical process. A Parameter entity bound to deviceId is the live/materialized value for a specific Device: factory value, current value, locally tuned value, HMI-modified value, or value loaded from a DeviceConfig.
Loading a DeviceConfig into a Device copies, maps, writes or materializes Parameter values from configuration scope into device scope. After that load, device-bound values may legitimately diverge because of manual changes, automatic tuning, maintenance activities, PLC/HMI changes, service operations or emergency interventions.
Compliance and drift checks compare configuration-bound Parameter values against device-bound Parameter values using DeviceRule tolerance fields and ParameterDef metadata.

entity.description:
Named operational plan for a root Device.
A DeviceRuleSet represents a production cycle, batch procedure, commissioning sequence, diagnostic sequence, validation plan or system-engineering procedure. It is anchored to one root deviceId. The root may be a physical/logical device or a DeviceGroup/system node.
If the root deviceId identifies a physical/logical device, every DeviceRule in the set must target that same device. If the root deviceId identifies a DeviceGroup/system node, every DeviceRule in the set must target the root itself or a physical/logical device that belongs to that group, including nested members where the implementation supports recursive expansion.
DeviceRuleSet is now the composition mechanism for applying multiple DeviceConfig records over one device or over a system hierarchy.

field.name: parentRuleSetId
field.description: Optional parent DeviceRuleSet used for inheritance, cloning lineage or composing higher-level procedures. A child rule set may specialize a base cycle for a product variant, plant variant, diagnostic mode or commissioning variant.

field.name: deviceId
field.description:
Root target Device for this rule set.
This field defines the scope boundary of the complete DeviceRuleSet. If it points to a physical/logical device, all child DeviceRule rows must target that same device. If it points to a DeviceGroup/system node, child DeviceRule rows must target the root group or devices that are members of that group according to DeviceGroupMember and/or the Device hierarchy.

field.name: sequenceNum
field.description: Execution order among sibling sets.

moqui.basic.Enumeration.description: Device Process Rule Set (a sequence of device configurations)

entity.description:
One operational step inside a DeviceRuleSet.
A DeviceRule applies, checks, asserts, suggests or validates one atomic DeviceConfig against one target Device. The rule belongs to exactly one DeviceRuleSet and is constrained by the root DeviceRuleSet.deviceId.
If the parent DeviceRuleSet targets a physical/logical device, this rule must target the same device. If the parent DeviceRuleSet targets a DeviceGroup/system node, this rule may target the root group itself when the operation is group-level, or a physical/logical member device inside that group. This prevents a cycle for one machine, cell or plant area from accidentally operating on devices outside its root scope.
DeviceRule.priority is the logical execution step inside the DeviceRuleSet. Rules with the same priority belong to the same phase and may be applied, checked or validated together. Increasing priority values represent progression through commissioning, startup, production phases, batch phases, validation, compliance checking, recovery or shutdown.
DeviceRuleSet + DeviceRule.priority is the model's composition mechanism for system-level and batch-level configuration.

field.name: parentRuleId
field.description: For hierarchical rules.

field.name: ruleTypeEnumId
field.description: Type of operation performed by this rule: apply a configuration, suggest a configuration, assert expected values, check compliance, or perform soft validation.

field.name: deviceRuleSetId
field.description: Parent DeviceRuleSet. The parent rule set defines the root scope for this rule and constrains the allowed target deviceId.

field.name: deviceConfigId
field.description:
DeviceConfig operated on by this rule.
The referenced DeviceConfig supplies the declared parameter values, control method and/or trajectory used by apply/check/assert/suggest/validate operations. Runtime services should validate compatibility between the DeviceConfig.deviceTypeEnumId and the target Device.deviceTypeEnumId.

field.name: deviceId
field.description:
Target Device for this rule.
This target must be within the root scope declared by DeviceRuleSet.deviceId. If the parent rule set targets a physical/logical device, this value must normally equal the parent rule set deviceId. If the parent rule set targets a DeviceGroup/system node, this value must normally identify the root group or one of its member devices, including nested members when recursive group expansion is supported.

field.name: statusId
field.description: The status to which this rule can be applied.

field.name: statusFlowId
field.description: The status flow to which this rule can be applied.

field.name: priority
field.description:
Logical execution priority within the DeviceRuleSet.
Rules with the same priority belong to the same logical phase and may be applied, checked, asserted, suggested or validated together. Increasing priority values represent progression through commissioning, startup, production phases, batch phases, subsystem configuration, validation, compliance checking, recovery or shutdown.
Priority is the sequencing mechanism for composing multiple DeviceConfig applications inside a rule set.

field.name: runDevice
field.description: Run the device after applying the configuration. If set to false the device will not be called.

field.name: floatingPointTolerance
field.description: Floating point comparison tolerance.

field.name: timeTolerance
field.description: Time comparison tolerance.

entity.description: The associative binding entity between vector trajectory and the actual parameters of the devices that host its components.
